titleStmt.title: Letter from Andrew Philip Magill to Thomas Henry Grattan Esmonde, 19 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Andrew Philip Magill

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by Andrew Philip Magill (1871-1941) to Thomas Henry Grattan Esmonde (1862-1935). Magill was a civil servant and held the position of private secretary for successive chief secretaries of Ireland until his transfer to the Northern Irish Civil Service in 1921. Esmonde was a landowner and politician. The letter refers to a testimonial written by the Archbishop of Tuam, John Healy (1841-1918), recommending that Sir John Robert O'Connell (1868-1943), a solicitor, be chosen for the vacant position of chief crown solicitor for Ireland.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/7153

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Copy 19th April, 1916. Dear Sir Thomas Esmonde, I am desired by Mr. Birrell to acknowledge the
receipt of your letter of 16th instant forwarding
copy of a letter from the Archbishop of Tuam recommending
Sir J. R. O'Connell for appointment as Chief Crown Solicitor
for Ireland, which will receive attention. Yours faithfully, (Sd.) A.P.M. Under Secy.
A.P.M
19/4/16.
Sir Thomas H. Grattan Esmonde, Bart., M.P., Ballynastragh,
Inch, Co.Wexford
16.4.16 Dear Mr Birrell Here is a copy of a letter
Sir John O'Connell has received from
the Archbishop of Tuam, with
forwarding letter to me. SnyYours Thos. H. Grattan Esmonde.
Copy Dr Healy's Letter 34, Kildare Street,
Dublin. St Jarlaths, Tuam April 8 I am acquainted with Sir
John R.O'Connell for
several years and I have
found him to be an able
and accomplished
gentleman eminently
fit to succeed to Sir M
Kelly in his office. I hope he
will be successful in his
candidature. John Healy DD
Archbishop of Tuam
